tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I'm working on a history paper and I was wondering if you already have any information saved from before, or do we need to start fresh?
Let me check if there's any stored information from previous conversations.

It looks like we need to start fresh! I don't have any stored information from previous conversations. There are no memories saved yet, so we'll be building from scratch.

What would you like to work on for your history paper? I'd be happy to help you get started!

Oh perfect, that makes sense! So I'm working on a history paper about the causes of the American Civil War. I want to focus especially on the economic differences between the North and South, and maybe touch on the slavery aspect too.

My teacher said we need at least three primary sources and five secondary sources. I've already found a couple books at the library that seem helpful - one is about industrial economics in the 1850s and another is about plantation records from Georgia.

Could you save all this information so I don't have to repeat myself later? It would be really helpful to have it stored somewhere for when I come back to work on this.
Absolutely! Let me save all of that information for you.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "History paper topic: Causes of the American Civil War, focusing on economic differences between North and South, and touching on slavery. Teacher requirement: at least 3 primary sources and 5 secondary sources. Sources already found: 1) Book about industrial economics in the 1850s, 2) Book about plantation records from Georgia."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "History paper topic: Causes of the American Civil War, focusing on economic differences between North and South, and touching on slavery. Teacher requirement: at least 3 primary sources and 5 secondary sources. Sources already found: 1) Book about industrial economics in the 1850s, 2) Book about plantation records from Georgia."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've saved all the key details:

- **Paper topic**: Causes of the American Civil War, focusing on economic differences between North and South, plus slavery
- **Source requirements**: 3+ primary sources, 5+ secondary sources
- **Books you've found**: One on industrial economics in the 1850s, one on Georgia plantation records

I'll remember all of this for when you come back. If you find more sources or want to add any other details, just let me know and I can update it. Good luck with the research!
Thanks for saving all that! Just to make sure it worked - if I come back later and ask about my history paper, you'll have all this information ready to go, right?
Let me double-check that it's stored properly.

{"results": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "History paper topic: Causes of the American Civil War, focusing on economic differences between North and South, and touching on slavery. Teacher requirement: at least 3 primary sources and 5 secondary sources. Sources already found: 1) Book about industrial economics in the 1850s, 2) Book about plantation records from Georgia."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
Yes, it's all saved and working! I just checked, and your information is stored. When you come back and ask about your history paper, I'll have everything ready:

- Your topic (American Civil War causes, focusing on North vs. South economics and slavery)
- The source requirements (3 primary, 5 secondary sources)
- The two books you've already found

You're all set—see you next time!

structured_turns.goal_text: Check what memories the assistant already has stored about the user or their history paper research
structured_turns.user_message: Hey, I'm working on a history paper and I was wondering if you already have any information saved from before, or do we need to start fresh?
structured_turns.assistant_message:
It looks like we need to start fresh! I don't have any stored information from previous conversations. There are no memories saved yet, so we'll be building from scratch.

What would you like to work on for your history paper? I'd be happy to help you get started!

structured_turns.goal_text: Store important information about the history paper topic, sources, and preferences for future reference
structured_turns.user_message:
Oh perfect, that makes sense! So I'm working on a history paper about the causes of the American Civil War. I want to focus especially on the economic differences between the North and South, and maybe touch on the slavery aspect too.

My teacher said we need at least three primary sources and five secondary sources. I've already found a couple books at the library that seem helpful - one is about industrial economics in the 1850s and another is about plantation records from Georgia.

Could you save all this information so I don't have to repeat myself later? It would be really helpful to have it stored somewhere for when I come back to work on this.

Done! I've saved all the key details:

- **Paper topic**: Causes of the American Civil War, focusing on economic differences between North and South, plus slavery
- **Source requirements**: 3+ primary sources, 5+ secondary sources

I'll remember all of this for when you come back. If you find more sources or want to add any other details, just let me know and I can update it. Good luck with the research!
